title: ADA 2026: Baseline Activity and Break Up Sedentary Time
summary: Evaluate baseline activity/sedentary time and counsel people with diabetes to interrupt prolonged sitting at least every 30 minutes.
source: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, Section 5 Recommendation 5.34

title: ADA 2026: Weekly Aerobic Activity Target
summary: Most adults with diabetes should perform at least 150 minutes/week of moderate-to-vigorous aerobic activity over 3 or more days, with no more than 2 consecutive inactive days.
source: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, Section 5 Recommendation 5.36

title: ADA 2026: Sleep Health Screening and Sleep-Promoting Habits
summary: Screen for sleep health in people with (or at risk for) diabetes and counsel sleep-promoting routines and habits.
source: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, Section 5 Recommendations 5.56 and 5.57

title: ADA 2026: A1C Goal for Many Nonpregnant Adults
summary: An A1C goal of under 7% is appropriate for many nonpregnant adults, individualized to hypoglycemia risk, comorbidities, and treatment burden.
source: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, Section 6 Recommendation 6.3a

title: ADA 2026: Peak Postprandial Glucose Target
summary: For many nonpregnant adults, a typical peak postprandial capillary glucose target is under 180 mg/dL (measured about 1-2 hours after meal start).
source: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, Section 6 Table 6.3

title: ADA 2026: Hypoglycemia Immediate Treatment
summary: For conscious individuals with glucose under 70 mg/dL, use fast-acting glucose/carbohydrate and recheck in 15 minutes; repeat treatment if still low.
source: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, Section 6 Recommendation 6.15
